subject: History
chapter_name: Tribals, Dikus and the Vision of a Golden Age
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the social and economic life of tribal communities
Explain the meaning and role of dikus in tribal areas
Analyse the impact of British forest laws on tribal life
Understand the leadership of Birsa Munda
Explain the concept of the Vision of a Golden Age
Identify the causes and consequences of tribal revolts

lesson_plan.title: Tribal Communities and Their Lifestyle
teaching_strategy: Use of pictures of tribal life
student_activities: Locate major tribal regions on a map
List economic activities of tribal communities
Discuss how forests supported tribal livelihood

lesson_plan.title: Tribal Society and Occupations
student_activities: Prepare a list of tribal occupations
Write a short note on the importance of forests in tribal life

lesson_plan.title: Dikus and Their Influence
topics_covered: Entry of outsiders into tribal areas
Impact of traders, moneylenders, and landlords
student_activities: Explain the term Diku in your own words
Prepare a cause–effect chart showing problems faced by tribals

lesson_plan.title: Birsa Munda and Tribal Resistance
student_activities: Write five key points about Birsa Munda
Prepare a short timeline of the Munda movement

lesson_plan.title: Vision of a Golden Age
topics_covered: Birsa’s idea of a Golden Age
student_activities: Write a short explanation of Birsa’s vision of a Golden Age
Discuss why tribals supported Birsa Munda’s movement

lesson_plan.title: Tribal Revolts and Chapter Revision
topics_covered: Impact of colonial policies on tribals
student_activities: Prepare a flowchart showing causes of tribal revolts
Participate in a chapter revision quiz

teaching_aids: Pictures or charts of tribal life
evaluation_methods: Participation in map and activity work
teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect tribal history with geography and environment so they understand how forests, land, and colonial policies affected tribal communities.
